items.q: What yellow arcade character eats pellets in a maze?
items.explanation: Namco designed Pac-Man as a maze chase game with broad appeal, helping it become one of the defining arcade hits of the 1980s.

items.q: Checkmate ends a game of chess immediately.
items.explanation: Checkmate means the king is under attack and no legal move can remove the threat, so the game is over on that move.

items.q: Which company produces the PlayStation console line?
items.explanation: Sony entered the console market with the original PlayStation in the mid-1990s and turned it into one of gaming's biggest brands.

items.q: What board game asks players to build words from letter tiles on a grid?
items.explanation: Premium spaces such as Double Word Score make board placement as important as vocabulary in Scrabble.

items.q: What video game places players in a block-built world with Survival and Creative modes?
items.explanation: Minecraft began as an indie sandbox project before Mojang expanded it into one of the best-selling games ever made.

items.q: Which color moves first in chess?
items.explanation: White moving first became the modern standard in organized chess and shaped how opening theory is discussed.

items.q: A standard deck of playing cards has four suits.
items.explanation: Those suits are hearts, diamonds, clubs, and spades, with 13 cards in each suit in a standard 52-card deck.

items.q: What board game has players try to sink an opponent's fleet on a grid?
items.explanation: Battleship began as a pencil-and-paper guessing game before becoming the familiar peg-and-grid tabletop version.

items.q: What is the name of Mario's brother in Nintendo games?
items.explanation: Luigi first appeared alongside Mario in the 1983 arcade game Mario Bros. and later starred in series such as Luigi's Mansion.

items.q: What tabletop game asks players to remove wooden blocks from a tower without making it collapse?
items.explanation: Jenga starts with a tower of stacked wooden blocks, and tension rises as the structure becomes less stable with each turn.

items.q: What kingdom is the main setting of The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild?
items.explanation: Breath of the Wild presents a ruined version of Hyrule and lets players approach its major objectives in a highly flexible order.

items.q: The Nintendo 64 used cartridges instead of optical discs for its games.
items.explanation: Nintendo kept cartridges for the N64, trading away CD storage capacity in exchange for fast load times and harder-to-copy media.

items.q: Who created the Metal Gear series?
items.explanation: Metal Gear became one of the medium's defining stealth series and is also known for its dense cinematic storytelling.

items.q: What board game uses tiles to build roads, cities, and cloisters across a medieval landscape?
items.explanation: Carcassonne helped popularize tile-laying design and the nickname meeple for the small wooden followers used to claim features.

items.q: What resource do mountain hexes produce in Catan?
items.explanation: Ore becomes especially valuable later in Catan because cities and development cards both rely heavily on it.

items.q: What resource fuels minion and spell plays in Hearthstone?
items.explanation: Hearthstone adds one mana crystal per turn up to ten, creating the game's built-in curve for early and late plays.

items.q: In standard Sudoku, each row must contain the digits 1 through 9 exactly once.
items.explanation: The same uniqueness rule also applies to each column and each 3x3 box, which is why one entry can constrain multiple regions at once.

items.q: What is the term for finishing a game as quickly as possible, often with optimized routing?
items.explanation: Speedrunning communities usually split records into categories such as any%, glitchless, and 100% so runs can be compared fairly.

items.q: Eric Barone is better known by what developer alias?
items.explanation: Barone spent years building Stardew Valley largely on his own, covering design, art, music, and programming under the ConcernedApe name.

items.q: In Dungeons & Dragons 5e, what term means rolling two d20s and using the higher result?
items.explanation: Advantage and disadvantage simplify many modifiers in 5e by changing how dice are rolled instead of stacking lots of small bonuses.

items.q: What island province is the main setting of The Elder Scrolls III: Morrowind?
items.explanation: Vvardenfell is dominated by Red Mountain and rival Great Houses, giving Morrowind its distinctive ash-heavy landscape and politics.

items.q: In Go, komi is added to White's score before territory is counted.
items.explanation: Komi compensates for Black moving first, and different rulesets vary slightly in the exact fractional value they award.

items.q: Who designed the board game Terraforming Mars?
items.explanation: Terraforming Mars became a major hobby hit for its engine-building structure and card synergies tied to science, cities, and global parameters.

items.q: What card type introduced in Lorwyn represents planeswalking mages in Magic: The Gathering?
items.explanation: Planeswalkers enter with loyalty counters and usually activate one loyalty ability per turn under their controller's command.

items.q: What does STAB stand for in competitive Pokemon discussions?
items.explanation: Moves that match a Pokemon's type receive a power boost, making STAB central to damage calculations and moveset planning.

items.q: What parasitic species is directed by the Gravemind in Halo?
items.explanation: The Flood spreads by infecting hosts, while the Gravemind acts as a shared intelligence capable of coordinating large outbreaks.

items.q: Guile's Sonic Boom in Street Fighter II is a charge move.
items.explanation: Players hold back before pressing forward and punch, which gives Guile a very different rhythm from quarter-circle characters such as Ryu.

items.q: What real-time strategy game features the races Protoss, Terran, and Zerg?
items.explanation: StarCraft became a foundational esports title because its three factions were highly asymmetrical but still competitively balanced.

items.q: Matt Leacock designed what cooperative board game about containing worldwide disease outbreaks?
items.explanation: Pandemic helped popularize modern cooperative board game design by making players optimize shared actions against an escalating system.

items.q: What fighting-game term describes the number of frames before a move becomes active?
items.explanation: Players analyze startup to judge how quickly a move can interrupt pressure, punish mistakes, or win scrambles at close range.
